tagline: From a bachelor's degree to Principal Security Engineer — two promotions, ten years.

start.title: Security Engineer
start.note: You are here — a bachelor's degree, ~0 years of experience. You monitor, triage alerts, and implement the controls you're handed.

rungs.title: Senior Security Engineer
rungs.scope: Own a security domain end-to-end — detection & response, or the security of a whole product — instead of working tickets.
rungs.experience: ~5 years. You run incidents, threat-model real systems, and set the security bar for your area.
rungs.evaluated: Evidence you already operate at Senior scope: incidents you led, risks you drove down (measurably), and peers who trust your judgment on security calls.

rungs.title: Staff / Principal Security Engineer
rungs.scope: Set security direction several teams build against — architecture, standards, and a program that raises the whole org's security.
rungs.experience: ~9–12+ years. A promotion gated on org-wide security impact and architecture leadership, not tenure.
rungs.evaluated: Sustained org-wide impact: a security architecture teams build on, a program that measurably lowered risk, and Staff+ sponsors who attest to it.
